description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Oklahoma (  OH-klə-HOH-mə; Choctaw: Oklahumma, pronounced [oklahómma]) is a landlocked state in the South Central and Southwestern region of the United States.

description: city in Oklahoma, United States and county seat of Tulsa County
description_wiki: Tulsa (  TUL-sə) is the second-most-populous city in the U.S. state of Oklahoma and the 48th-most populous city in the United States.

description: city in Oklahoma, United States
description_wiki: Stillwater is the tenth-largest city in the U.S. state of Oklahoma, and the county seat of Payne County.

description: public university in Oklahoma, United States of America
description_wiki: Oklahoma State University (informally Oklahoma State or OSU) is a public land-grant research university in Stillwater, Oklahoma, United States.

description: city in Tulsa County, Oklahoma, United States of America
description_wiki: Jenks is a city in Tulsa County, Oklahoma, United States, and a suburb of Tulsa, in the northeastern part of the state.

description: private university in Cambridge, Massachusetts, USA, founded 1861
description_wiki: The Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) is a private research university in Cambridge, Massachusetts, United States.

description: city in Missouri, United States
description_wiki: Carthage is a city in Jasper County, Missouri, United States.

description: county seat of Fresno County, California, United States
description_wiki: Fresno ( ; Spanish for 'ash tree') is a city in the San Joaquin Valley of California, United States.

description: Wikimedia disambiguation page
description_wiki: Francis of Frank Phillips may refer to:

description: city in southern Jasper County and northern Newton County, Missouri, USA
description_wiki: Joplin is a city in Jasper and Newton counties in the U.S. state of Missouri.

description: United States federal government public work relief program part of the New Deal
description_wiki: The Civilian Conservation Corps (CCC) was a government work relief program that ran from 1933 to 1942 in the United States for unemployed, unmarried men ages 18–25 and eventually expanded to ages 17–28, who volunteered amid widespread unemployment.

description: maritime land force service branch of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United States Marine Corps (USMC), also referred to as the United States Marines or simply the Marines, is the naval infantry service branch of the United States Armed Forces.

aliases: Fort Kamehameha Flying Field Tracts A and B
description: United States Air Force base at Honolulu International Airport, Hawaii, USA
1941 Japanese surprise attack on the U.S
description_wiki: On December 7, 1941, the Empire of Japan launched a surprise military strike on the United States Pacific Fleet at its naval base at Pearl Harbor on Oahu, Hawaii.

surface_forms: 6th and Main Street, there in Joplin
description: city in southern Jasper County and northern Newton County, Missouri, USA
description_wiki: Joplin is a city in Jasper and Newton counties in the U.S. state of Missouri.

description: city in and county seat of Leavenworth County, Kansas, United States
description_wiki: Leavenworth () is the county seat of and the largest city in Leavenworth County, Kansas, United States.

aliases: Ground forces of the United States Armed Forces
description: land service branch of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United States Army (U.S. Army) is the land service branch of the United States Armed Forces.

description: US Army training installation during World War II in Texas
description_wiki: Camp Barkeley was a large United States Army training installation during World War II. The base was located eleven miles (18 km) southwest of Abilene, Texas, near what is now Dyess Air Force Base.

description: chain of islands in the Northern Pacific
description_wiki: The Aleutian Islands (   ə-LOO-shən; Russian: Алеутские острова, romanized: Aleutskiye ostrova; Aleut: Unangam Tanangin, "land of the Aleuts"; possibly from the Chukchi aliat, or "island")—also called the Aleut Islands, Aleutic Islands, or, before the Alaska Purchase in 1867, the Catherine Archipelago—are a chain of 14 main, larger volcanic islands and 55 smaller ones.

description: US Marine Corps base near Oceanside, California, United States
description_wiki: Marine Corps Base Camp Pendleton is the major West Coast base of the United States Marine Corps and is one of the largest Marine Corps bases in the United States.
